output.summary: 9 bug fixes for the Header block: CSS hover effects for CTA link and search button, dropdown min-width constraint, fixed header animation and offset fixes, dropdown pseudo-element width scoping, and 3 keyboard navigation fixes (focus outline persistence, arrow key into dropdown, arrow key within dropdown).

fileChanges.description: Fix CTA hover, search button hover, fixed header width transition, and fixed header top offset
fileChanges.details: 1. CTA link (line 114): Change `hover:opacity-60` to `hover:text-accent` so hover matches menu items instead of opacity fade. Same for mobile CTA button (line 45). 2. Search submit button (lines 88-93 and 162-166): Add `hover:text-accent transition-colors` class so the search icon changes to accent on hover. 3. Fixed header `.header-main` class (line 54): The class `transition-all` causes width to animate when becoming fixed. Change to `transition-[transform,opacity]` (or more specific properties) to exclude width from transitions. 4. Fixed header offset: Replace `top-(--fixed-offset)` with `top-8` (32px) everywhere AND keep admin bar awareness. Best approach: In the JS, set `--fixed-offset` to 32px (or 32 + adminBarHeight). Currently calculateFixedOffset sums up sibling fixed elements but doesn't add the 32px gap. Add 32px to the offset value in JS.

fileChanges.description: Sync TSX hover classes with PHP changes
fileChanges.details: 1. CTA link: Change `hover:opacity-60` to `hover:text-accent` (both desktop and mobile CTA). 2. Search button: Add `hover:text-accent transition-colors`. 3. Fixed header: Change `transition-all` to `transition-[transform,opacity]`. These are editor-only visual sync changes.

fileChanges.description: Fix dropdown min-width and pseudo-element width
fileChanges.details: 1. Dropdown min-width (line 55): The submenu currently uses `md:min-w-max` which sizes to content. Need to add a min-width that accounts for the parent item width + padding. Use `md:min-w-[calc(100%+3rem)]` on the submenu (the 3rem = 1.5rem padding on each side from `md:p-6`, plus parent item width via 100% of `.header-main-item`). Actually since the dropdown is absolutely positioned, 100% refers to the parent li. So `md:min-w-full` should ensure it's at least as wide as the parent. Combined with `md:-ml-6` offset, we need `md:min-w-[calc(100%+3rem)]` to cover the parent width + horizontal padding. 2. Pseudo-element width (line 55): Replace `before:md:left-0 before:md:right-0` with `before:md:w-full` so the hover bridge matches the dropdown width rather than stretching to viewport edges. Since the pseudo-element is on an absolutely positioned container, `left-0 right-0` already refers to the dropdown's own edges, but if the dropdown has padding or the element is positioned differently, use `before:md:inset-x-0` which is equivalent but more explicit. Actually, reviewing the code: the pseudo-element is on the `.header-main-item-submenu` div which is `md:absolute`. The `before:md:left-0 before:md:right-0` creates a pseudo positioned relative to the submenu div. This should already match the dropdown width. The issue may be that the `before` pseudo needs `before:md:absolute` to actually be positioned (it already has it). Let me re-examine: `before:md:absolute before:md:bottom-full before:md:left-0 before:md:right-0 before:md:h-6` - this IS relative to the submenu, so it should match width. The user reports it stretches full width, which suggests the submenu itself or pseudo has a positioning context issue. Fix: ensure the submenu has `md:relative` (no, it already has `md:absolute`). The pseudo with `absolute` positioning and `left-0 right-0` on an `absolute` parent should work. May need to add explicit `before:md:w-full before:md:left-auto before:md:right-auto` or simply change to `before:md:inset-x-0 before:md:w-full` and remove left-0/right-0. Alternatively, the simplest fix: remove `before:md:left-0 before:md:right-0` and use `before:md:w-full before:md:left-0` only.

fileChanges.description: Sync TSX dropdown classes with PHP changes
fileChanges.details: Mirror the dropdown min-width and pseudo-element width changes from the PHP template.

fileChanges.description: Fix keyboard navigation: focus outline persistence, ArrowDown/Up from trigger into dropdown, and Up/Down inside dropdown
fileChanges.details:
ANALYSIS OF KEYBOARD ISSUES:

**Issue #7 - Focus outline blinks instead of persisting:**
The CSS uses `focus-visible:outline` classes. The issue is that `focus-visible` in browsers may not persist when navigating with arrow keys if the element loses and regains focus quickly, OR the `transition-colors` class on the button is transitioning the outline property. Fix: The items have `transition-colors` which should NOT affect outline. But reviewing the submenu button (HeaderMainItem.php line 39), it has `transition-colors` which is fine. The blink may be caused by the dropdown opening/closing resetting focus. Check if `openSubmenu()` or DOM changes are causing focus loss. Actually the most likely cause: when the button has `transition-all` or the submenu show/hide causes a repaint. The fix should ensure that `focus-visible:outline` uses `outline` which is NOT part of `transition-colors`. This should work. If the outline is blinking, it may be because of some global CSS resetting outline. Add `focus-visible:outline-2! focus-visible:outline-white! focus-visible:outline-offset-2!` with important flags, OR check if there's a global transition that includes outline.

Actual root cause likely: The `transition-colors` on the button includes outline-color transitions in Tailwind v4. Tailwind v4's `transition-colors` transitions: `color, background-color, border-color, text-decoration-color, fill, stroke, outline-color`. The outline-color transition causes the outline to fade in/out. Fix: add `outline-color-transparent` as base state and `focus-visible:outline-white` with the transition causing it to fade. Remove `transition-colors` from outline-affecting elements or add `transition-none` for outline specifically. Best fix: Override with `outline-0` as default and `focus-visible:outline-2` to ensure it snaps on, or add `transition-[color]` instead of `transition-colors` to exclude outline-color. Simplest: add `outline-transparent` to the base state so the transition animates from transparent to white (but still shows), OR change to just `transition-color` (not `transition-colors`).

**Issue #8 - ArrowDown/Up from trigger doesn't move focus into dropdown:**
Looking at the JS code (lines 409-456), the button keydown handler DOES handle ArrowDown (line 414-421) and ArrowUp (line 422-429). It opens the submenu and calls `items[0]?.focus()` or `items[items.length-1]?.focus()`. The `items` array is populated at line 351: `const items = Array.from(submenu.querySelectorAll('[role="menuitem"]'))`. If items is empty, focus won't move. The issue is likely that when the submenu is `hidden` (has class `hidden` = `display:none`), the querySelectorAll for `[role="menuitem"]` returns elements but they can't receive focus because they're in a hidden container. The `items` array is built once during `handleSubmenus()` init, so the elements exist. But `focus()` on an element inside `display:none` does nothing. The fix: the `openSubmenu()` function removes `hidden` and adds `block`, but the `items[0]?.focus()` call happens in the same synchronous block. The browser may not have reflowed yet. Fix: wrap `items[0]?.focus()` in a `requestAnimationFrame` or `setTimeout(fn, 0)` to allow the DOM to update before focusing.

**Issue #9 - Up/Down arrows don't change selection inside dropdown:**
The submenu keydown handler is at lines 354-403, attached to `submenu.addEventListener('keydown', handleSubmenuKeydown)`. It checks `button.getAttribute('aria-expanded') === 'true'` first (line 355). The handler navigates items with ArrowUp/Down. If this isn't working, the issue may be that focus never reaches the submenu items (related to #8), so the keydown never fires on the submenu element. Or the `items` list is stale. If #8 is fixed (focus actually moves to items), then #9 should work since the event listener IS on the submenu and delegates to items. Verify that the `items` selection `submenu.querySelectorAll('[role="menuitem"]')` matches the actual sub-item elements. Looking at HeaderMainSubItem.php: the `<li>` has `role="menuitem"` (line 17). The submenu container has a `<ul>` and the `<li role="menuitem">` elements inside. The querySelectorAll on the submenu div WILL find these. So #9 is likely a cascading issue from #8.

designDecisions.decision: CTA link hover uses text-accent color instead of opacity reduction
designDecisions.evidence: User requested: 'External CTA link should change to accent color on hover (same as menu items)'. Menu items already use hover:text-accent.

designDecisions.decision: Search submit button hover uses text-accent color
designDecisions.evidence: User requested: 'Search submit button should change to accent color on hover'

designDecisions.decision: Dropdown min-width set to parent item width plus horizontal padding
designDecisions.evidence: User requested: 'Dropdown should be at least the full width of its parent item plus its horizontal padding'

designDecisions.decision: Fixed header uses specific transition properties excluding width to prevent width animation
designDecisions.evidence: User reported: 'There is a width change animation when fixing the header — it should not happen'

designDecisions.decision: Fixed header top offset is 32px from top (plus admin bar height if present)
designDecisions.evidence: User requested: 'When fixed, header should be 32px from the top (or offset below the admin bar if present)'

designDecisions.decision: Dropdown hover bridge pseudo-element width matches dropdown, not full viewport width
designDecisions.evidence: User reported: 'The hover bridge pseudo-element should match the dropdown width'

designDecisions.decision: Focus outline persists while element has focus, not just blinks
designDecisions.evidence: User reported: 'Visible outline just blinks instead of staying'. Root cause: Tailwind v4 transition-colors includes outline-color, causing outline to transition/fade.

designDecisions.decision: ArrowDown/Up on menu button trigger moves focus into dropdown items
designDecisions.evidence: User reported: 'Focus does not move into dropdown items when pressing ArrowDown/ArrowUp on a menu button'. Root cause: focus() called on hidden element before DOM reflow.

designDecisions.decision: Up/Down arrows cycle through items inside open dropdown
designDecisions.evidence: User reported: 'Up/Down arrows do not change selected element inside the dropdown'. Likely cascading from #8 — once focus enters the dropdown, the existing keydown handler should work.

issue1_cta_hover.change: Replace `hover:opacity-60` with `hover:text-accent` on both the desktop CTA link (line 114 PHP) and the mobile CTA button above the navbar (line 45 PHP). The mobile CTA in the footer section (line 187 PHP) should also get hover:text-accent if interactive.

issue2_search_hover.change: Add `hover:text-accent transition-colors` to the search submit `<button>` element class. Both desktop (line 90 PHP) and mobile (line 163 PHP) search buttons.

issue3_dropdown_min_width.change: On the `.header-main-item-submenu` div, replace or supplement `md:min-w-max` with `md:min-w-[calc(100%+3rem)]` where 3rem = 2 * 1.5rem (the p-6 horizontal padding). This ensures the dropdown is at least as wide as the parent `<li>` + its own padding.

issue4_fixed_header_animation.change: Replace `transition-all` with `transition-[transform,opacity]` on the `.header-main` div. This prevents width from being animated when the header becomes fixed and changes from natural width to --max-container.
issue4_fixed_header_animation.risk: medium — verify other transitions (like hide-on-scroll-down translate) still work

issue5_fixed_header_offset.change: In `calculateFixedOffset()`, ensure the offset always includes 32px base gap. Currently it only sums sibling fixed element heights + admin bar. Change to: `offset = 32 + adminBarHeight + siblingFixedHeight`. This makes `--fixed-offset` = 32px + admin bar (typically 32px on desktop, 46px on mobile). The CSS `top-(--fixed-offset)` then positions correctly.
issue5_fixed_header_offset.risk: medium — test with and without admin bar, and with other fixed siblings

issue6_pseudo_element_width.change: The `before:` pseudo-element on the submenu uses `before:md:absolute before:md:bottom-full before:md:left-0 before:md:right-0 before:md:h-6`. Since the submenu is `md:absolute`, the pseudo should be relative to it. If it's stretching beyond, the issue may be that the `.header-main-item` (the `<li>`) is the containing block, not the submenu itself. Fix: add `md:relative` to the submenu div is wrong since it's already absolute. Actually, the `before:` pseudo with `position:absolute` inherits the positioning context of the `.header-main-item-submenu` element. If that element is `position: absolute` (from `md:absolute`), the pseudo's containing block is the submenu's offset parent (the `<li>` with `relative`). So `left-0 right-0` on the pseudo spans the full `<li>` width, not the submenu width. Fix: remove `before:md:left-0 before:md:right-0` and instead use `before:md:inset-x-0` after making the submenu itself the positioning context by adding an explicit `before:md:left-0 before:md:right-0` relative to the dropdown. Actually the simplest fix: add `md:isolate` or make the pseudo width match by using `before:md:w-full` with `before:md:left-0` only, and ensure the submenu has its own stacking context. The real fix: the `before` pseudo is a child of the submenu div which has `position:absolute`. The `before` pseudo has `position:absolute`. Its containing block is the submenu div's padding box (since submenu is positioned). So `left:0; right:0` on the before should span the submenu width. BUT if the submenu has `overflow:visible` (default) and the before is outside its bounds (bottom-full), it still references the submenu edges. This should work correctly. Need to test and verify — it may be a browser quirk or the containing block might be wrong due to transform/will-change on an ancestor.

issue7_focus_outline.change: The `transition-colors` class in Tailwind v4 transitions `outline-color` among other properties. This causes the focus outline to fade in (appearing to blink). Fix: On all elements that have both `transition-colors` and `focus-visible:outline`, add `outline-transparent` as a base class so the transition goes from transparent -> white smoothly, OR replace `transition-colors` with a custom transition that excludes outline-color: `transition-[color,background-color,border-color,text-decoration-color,fill,stroke]`. The simplest approach: add `outline-0` as base and keep `focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-white` so the outline snaps to 2px on focus (outline-width is not transitioned by transition-colors, only outline-color is). Actually if outline-color transitions from currentColor to white it may cause a visible flash. Best fix: add `outline-transparent` to base state of interactive nav items so the color transition is transparent->white.

issue8_arrow_into_dropdown.change: In the button keydown handler for ArrowDown (line 414-421) and ArrowUp (line 422-429), after calling `openSubmenu(button)`, wrap the `items[0]?.focus()` / `items[items.length-1]?.focus()` call in `requestAnimationFrame(() => { ... })` to allow the DOM to reflow after removing the `hidden` class before attempting to focus the item.

issue9_arrow_inside_dropdown.change: This is likely a cascading issue from #8. Once focus successfully enters the dropdown (fix #8), the existing keydown handler on the submenu element (lines 354-403) should handle ArrowUp/Down correctly. However, verify after #8 fix. If still broken, the issue may be that the `items` array was populated when elements were `display:none` and `querySelectorAll` returned them but they have no layout. Re-query items when the submenu opens. Fix: move the `items` query inside `handleSubmenuKeydown` so it's always fresh, or re-query after opening.
